title: Osmium Rerank Labs
preview:
Background: Osmium builds a federated search + rerank platform for regulated verticals (legal + compliance). They ingest ~10M docs today, expect 2-3x growth over 12 months. Currently using Pinecone for vectors + custom transformer reranker hosted on infra. Eng team is focused on reducing cost-per-query while holding or improving ranking metrics (MRR, nDCG).

Primary ask: migration plan for embeddings + reranker that preserves quality. They want evals-driven rollout: run comparative embedding+reranker evaluation harness (nDCG, MRR, recall@k, latency regression checks) before flip. Need A/B + canary capability with automatic rollback on quality regressions.

Scale & Perf targets:
- Index: 10M docs now, plan to 25M in 12mo.
- Query load: 400-600 QPS peak, batchy writes during ingest windows.
- Latency SLO: reranked end-to-end P95 <= 150ms for first-pass + reranker; P99 spike tolerance with fallback.
- Cost target: reduce per-query cost by 30% vs current stack (embeddings + reranker combined).

Security & Compliance:
- Must support VPC/private control plane and KMS integration.
- SOC2 plus audit logging & retention controls. Data residency: US-only for certain clients. SSO/SAML required for admin console.

Technical preferences and constraints:
- Prefer quantized model variants for CPU fallback for cold queries.
- Want prefix/KV caching and dynamic batching to hit latency targets.
- Need evaluation harness integrated with Redwood Optimize to run continuous regression checks on gold prompt sets.
- Canary strategy: start 5-10% traffic to candidate reranker + embeddings for 2 weeks, monitor MRR/nDCG and latency; expand to 50% if stable, then full cutover.

Recent activity / timeline:
- 2025-07-21: Intro call (Priya) — product fit looks strong; customer shared sample eval sets.
- 2025-08-05: Deep dive with infra + ML engs (Ethan attended) — discussed ingest pipeline and index rebuild cadence.
- 2025-08-18: Security questionnaire submitted; infosec follow-up pending.
- 2025-08-28: POC scope agreed — run embedding comparison (current vs 3 candidate embed models) + reranker A/B for 100k eval queries. Drive: /drive/osmium/rdw_pricing_deck_v3.pdf.
- 2025-09-01: Pricing sketch shared; finance asks for clear cost-per-query numbers for three rollout scenarios. Fireflies ff_20250901_osmium_call_01 attached.

Concerns heard from CTO / ML lead: "We cannot accept even small negative delta in legal search recall — false negatives are catastrophic." Wants automatic canary rollback and immutable eval history for audits.

Next steps (short):
- Send technical onboarding checklist for dedicated pool + KMS details (owner: Ethan).
- Provision dedicated test pool for canary (timeline: 2 business days after security signoff).
- Run initial embed+rerank eval (target: 1 week) and share nDCG/MRR/latency deltas.

Notes style: fragments, internal shorthand, action items. Contact: CTO Maya Bennett (maya@osmium-rerank.ai).

title: Helio Financial
preview: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration changes, (2) RBAC separation of duties, and (3) export to SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls. Secondary requirements: deterministic stickiness for end-user assignment and guardrails to auto-stop/soft-stop on latency/error regressions.
text: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration changes, (2) RBAC separation of duties, and (3) export to SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls. Secondary requirements: deterministic stickiness for end-user assignment and guardrails to auto-stop/soft-stop on latency/error regressions.

title: SpringForge KBloom
preview:
Summary: SaaS helpdesk vendor building KB RAG to power triage + automated replies. Focus: embeddings + light reranking pipeline (dense retriever -> small LM reranker).

Key metrics/requirements:
- Indexing: ~1.0-1.5M unique passages (mix of short FAQs, long articles, chat transcripts).
- QPS: peak 500-800 QPS for query endpoints, sustained 300-500 QPS during business hours.
- Latency targets: p50 <60ms, p95 <250ms for end-to-end (embedding lookup + rerank + response assembly).
- Cost sensitivity: strong; target <$0.004 per query (inc. embeddings + rerank tokens).
- Model preferences: open models preferred; want ability to pin model versions; interested in 8-bit quantized options for reranker.
- Routing/fallback: want simple downgrade path (cheaper embedding-only fallbacks) during spikes.

What we did: POC run on hosted API using ada-like embedding model + rerank with smaller Llama variant. Achieved p50 latency goals but p95 exceeded at high concurrency. Redwood suggested batching and prefix KV cache to reduce tokens and latency. Diego provided kernel/quantization tuning doc.

Quotes: "We need predictable economics — spikes kill our margins. If you can show steady <$0.004/q at 500 QPS we can scale fast." — Head of Product.

Open security items: data residency clause for EU data, sample audit logs for SOC2 evidence, KMS integration detail.

Docs & artifacts: POC perf CSV, call transcripts, pricing draft.

Short-term next steps: Perf optimization follow-up (Redwood Optimize suggestions), legal residency clause, finalize pricing band for committed capacity or sustained discounts.

title: Midnight Pegasus Infer Labs
preview:
Summary: evaluating Redwood for millisecond-sensitive trading signal pipeline. Primary ask: sub-50ms p50 inference for short-context (<=64 tokens) reranking + chat ops at ~1k QPS sustained with spikes to 4k QPS. Cost sensitivity high — target unit cost < $0.0006/token equivalent for production. Interested in batching, KV/prefix caching, and aggressive quantization profiles.

Recent activities (timeline):
- 2025-01-20: Intro call (AE Jamie O., CTO A. Khatri). high-level requirements, shared architecture diagram (drive link).
- 2025-01-28: Deep-dive with infra (SE Priya) — discussed current on-prem DGX fleet, NIC topology, and firewall constraints. noted CPU+network egress concern.
- 2025-02-05: Security kickoff — provided SOC2 report; asked for SAML flow spec. (ticket SEC-2178).
- 2025-02-11: Benchmarking scoping call (Fireflies ff_20250211_7f9c). Agreed to 2-week perf POC using Redwood Dedicated with custom quantized weights.
- 2025-02-18: Submitted sample workloads + synthetic traffic profile (1k sustained QPS, 4k burst). Requested p50/p95/p99 traces and per-token CPU/GPU breakdown.
- 2025-02-24: Redwood test harness run #1 — initial numbers: p50 ~72ms on default model variant, p95 ~180ms. Notes: KV cache not enabled; batching conservative.

Technical requirements / constraints:
- Workload: reranking of candidate trading signals (top-5 -> scored), short generation for alerts (<=64 tokens).
- Latency SLO: p50 <=50ms, p95 <=120ms for reranking path.
- Throughput: baseline 1k QPS with scale-to-4k on market open. Bursty traffic pattern with 20s micro-bursts.
- Cost: must show quantization + batching strategy reduces unit cost by >3x vs baseline.
- Models: prefer open weights — planning to test Llama2-13B / Mistral-7B as fast variant. Open to Redwood-verified quantized artifacts (q4f16, q8_0).
- Runtime: prefer Dedicated but need private control plane + VPC peering into our account. On-prem fallback if networking prevents peering.
- Observability: token-level latency + per-request routing trace required (audit).

POC asks (explicit):
- Full perf benchmark with and without KV/prefix caching enabled.
- Show impact of continuous batching (1ms vs 8ms batching window) on p50/p95 and unit cost.
- Quantization matrix tests: FP16, q4f16, q8_0 on target GPUs; expected memory footprint and perf delta.
- Kernel profiling output (kernel selection + compiles) and list of fallback variants used during spikes.
- A sample routing policy that falls back to smaller model variant during capacity shortages and expected degradation curve.

Quotes / call excerpts:
- CTO A. Khatri: "We can tolerate small quality drop if it guarantees 2-3x cost savings and keeps p50 under 50ms during live trading windows."
- Infra lead: "If peering adds >20ms RTT we need on-prem option."

Decision criteria / evaluation rubric:
- Latency: primary (meets SLOs under synthetic bursts).
- Cost: unit economics at sustained QPS.
- Reliability: graceful fallback and routing, no single-point of failure.
- Security: SAML + KMS + audit logs must be demonstrable in POC.
- Operability: rollout controls (canary, A/B), traceability of model versions.

Next steps / action items:
- Redwood: schedule Dedicated perf POC (2w) with config template for batching windows and KV cache toggles (SE Priya to coordinate).
- MP: provide sanitized sample dataset and coarse-hourly traffic curves (completed).
- Security: exchange SAML metadata and KMS requirements (ticket SEC-2178).
- Commercial: draft 1-year committed Dedicated pricing with burst capacity options — link to pricing draft (drive).

Blockers (internal):
- Budget sign-off contingent on POC results (VP Eng).
- Network peering complexity for VPC — net team evaluating latency impact.
- On-prem GPU firmware/drivers may not support certain optimized kernels — potential incompat.

Sales notes / negotiating levers:
- Offer initial 30% pilot discount on Dedicated commit if POC demonstrates >=2x cost reduction via quantization/batching.
- Provide quickstart SSO + audit log playbook to accelerate security review.

Recent artifacts:
- Perf run 2025-02-24: drive:/logs/mp/perf-run-20250224.csv
- Sales deck: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B-pegasus-poc-pricing

Record owner notes: keep interaction granular; next check-in scheduled 2025-03-03 to go over POC config.

title: Kythera MedSearch
preview:
Post-demo silence. Demo attended and product fit was positive (search + rerank + chat for clinicians). Key asks: HIPAA controls, KMS, on-prem option or VPC, data residency in us-east, tokenization/PII redaction. They explicitly asked for quantized model recommendations and cost estimate for 1000 qps inference (typical clinician load).

Current state: Legal/InfoSec placed hold — heavy backlog + demands for SOC2 report copies and encryption-at-rest details. Internal champion (Head of Data Science) changed teams last month -> no clear owner to push pilot. Budget conversation paused due to org-wide hiring/budget freeze through Q2.

Competitive pressure: they have an existing relationship with Azure and are evaluating Anthropic for safety features. Prospect comment on demo: "liked the observability and rollout features" but: "we need security sign-off before procurement."

Suggested approach: send tailored security packet (SOC2 + architecture + KMS playbook), offer 6-week POC credits + technical onboarding support, loop in Director for exec-to-exec nudging. Leave cadence: 1x/month nurture with value emails, re-open in 2025-06.

title: Northbridge SparkWorks
preview: High-signal mid-market SaaS (customer engagement + agent assist). Product: omnichannel support + AI summarization. Currently prototype on OpenAI; evaluating Redwood first via Hosted API POC, then Dedicated for predictable throughput and lower unit costs. -- Discovery call 2025-01-22: AE Marcus, SE Priya, VPEng A. Ortiz. Customer quote: 'we need predictable pricing and regional isolation for EU clients'. -- Demo 2025-01-29: Hosted API chat demo (small sandbox). Customer measured baseline: p95 latency ~210ms for simple chat flows; throughput tested ~35 concurrent agents before queueing. Embedding pipeline: 1k docs/min requirement for nightly indexing. -- POC objectives: 1) match latency SLO (p95 <250ms for chat), 2) reduce token unit cost vs Hosted baseline, 3) support EU residency for subset of data, 4) evaluate autoscaling/burst behaviour. -- Cost-optimization workshop 2025-02-10 (SE-led): focused on batching, prefix/KV caching, and quantization profiles. Outputs: recommended batching window 8-12ms for user-facing chat to hit 25-30% lower per-token cost without noticeable latency impact; enable prefix KV cache on support dialogs -> expected 20% token savings on repeat flows; quantization profile (8-bit + per-layer calibration) for their chosen LLM candidate projected to reduce GPU memory footprint and MRRP by ~35% on Dedicated. Unit economics: modeled at 3 production routes (hosted fallback, dedicated steady state, dedicated burst) -> projected ARR impact: move to Dedicated + caching + quantization = ~45% reduction in variable inference spend vs Hosted-only at target volume (estimated). -- Security: insists on SSO, SOC2 attestation, KMS integration for keys, audit trail for PII. Legal asked for DPA + residency SLA. -- Pricing: initial Dedicated pricing request to finance (asked for 12-month committed tier + burst credits). Customer cost sensitivity: mid-high; willing to commit for predictable unit economics but needs clear math for break-even vs Hosted. -- Internal notes: product team cares about degradation policies; platform team wants canary and automatic fallback to hosted. They prefer Redwood Optimize for ongoing suggestions. -- Next: Priya to deliver quantitative POC report (latency curves, cost models, quantization verification) by 2025-02-17. Follow-up meeting booked 2025-02-24 to review Dedicated demo and final pricing options.

Follow-up meeting booked 2025-02-24 to review Dedicated demo and final pricing options.

title: Silverbloom SaaSWorks
preview:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood for customer-facing features: chat support assistant + internal doc search.
- Starting with Hosted API POC to validate latency & quality, then evaluate Dedicated (VPC) for production.

POC scope / success metrics:
- Hosted POC (2 weeks): integrate chat endpoint + embeddings for KB retrieval.
- Latency target: 95th <= 350ms for short chat (prompt+context <= 512 tokens) on hosted path.
- Throughput: ~120 QPS peak for embeddings ingestion during sync windows, lower concurrent for chat.
- Quality: >85% CSAT on triaged responses in sandbox.

Security / procurement asks (driving Dedicated interest):
- SOC2 Type II report required before procurement sign-off (Lena).
- Must support SSO/SAML (Okta) + SCIM provisioning.
- Audit logs: per-request logging (token/route level) and 365d retention; need exportable logs to their SIEM.
- Retention controls: retention policy per route, ability to purge PII on demand.
- KMS: customer-managed keys for Dedicated volume encryption preferred.
- Data residency: US-only processing for initial rollout.
- Network: VPC peering or private link for Dedicated; interested in diagram of egress flows and NAT handling.

Model & runtime preferences:
- Preference for open instruction-tuned models (Llama2-like family) with fallbacks to smaller quantized variants for cost-sensitive routes.
- Want routing policy: low-latency route for premium customers, cost route for background jobs.
- Need recommendations for batching/quantization to meet cost goals.

Commercial / pricing notes:
- Initial ARR target 120-360k depending on Dedicated discount and support SLA.
- Sensitive to egress/network charges; requested sample invoice for 6-month usage pattern.
- Interested in committed Dedicated tiers for predictable latency and throughput.

Next steps / action items:
- Send SOC2 attestation + security pack (Jordan) — due 2025-02-05.
- Priya to produce Dedicated VPC architecture diagram + KMS options (deliverable 2025-02-10).
- Work with procurement to confirm retention & purge SLAs; Lena to share internal questionnaire by 2025-02-06.
- Start hosted POC integration by 2025-02-07 once dev keys provisioned.

Conversation notes / quotes:
- Marcus (CTO): "We need predictable latency for support assistants — can't have noisy neighbors in production."
- Lena (Head of Security): "SOC2 and SSO are table stakes; beyond that it's logging and the ability to delete user data on request."

Risks / unknowns:
- POC success may not translate to Dedicated if VPC networking introduces latency or added overhead.
- Procurement timeline stretches if SOC2 evidence delayed.

Other links & artifacts:
- Hosted demo recording: drive:/RD-Demos/Silverbloom/hosted-demo-2025-01-22.mp4
- Pricing sketch: drive:/RD-Deals/Silverbloom/pricing-sketch-2025-01-22.xlsx

CRM-style short fragments:
- "Lena wants SOC2 + log export"
- "Marcus asked about token-level cost breakdowns"
- "Priya to run canary for embeddings latency"
text:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood for customer-facing features: chat support assistant + internal doc search.
- Starting with Hosted API POC to validate latency & quality, then evaluate Dedicated (VPC) for production.

POC scope / success metrics:
- Hosted POC (2 weeks): integrate chat endpoint + embeddings for KB retrieval.
- Latency target: 95th <= 350ms for short chat (prompt+context <= 512 tokens) on hosted path.
- Throughput: ~120 QPS peak for embeddings ingestion during sync windows, lower concurrent for chat.
- Quality: >85% CSAT on triaged responses in sandbox.

Security / procurement asks (driving Dedicated interest):
- SOC2 Type II report required before procurement sign-off (Lena).
- Must support SSO/SAML (Okta) + SCIM provisioning.
- Audit logs: per-request logging (token/route level) and 365d retention; need exportable logs to their SIEM.
- Retention controls: retention policy per route, ability to purge PII on demand.
- KMS: customer-managed keys for Dedicated volume encryption preferred.
- Data residency: US-only processing for initial rollout.
- Network: VPC peering or private link for Dedicated; interested in diagram of egress flows and NAT handling.

Model & runtime preferences:
- Preference for open instruction-tuned models (Llama2-like family) with fallbacks to smaller quantized variants for cost-sensitive routes.
- Want routing policy: low-latency route for premium customers, cost route for background jobs.
- Need recommendations for batching/quantization to meet cost goals.

Commercial / pricing notes:
- Initial ARR target 120-360k depending on Dedicated discount and support SLA.
- Sensitive to egress/network charges; requested sample invoice for 6-month usage pattern.
- Interested in committed Dedicated tiers for predictable latency and throughput.

Next steps / action items:
- Send SOC2 attestation + security pack (Jordan) — due 2025-02-05.
- Priya to produce Dedicated VPC architecture diagram + KMS options (deliverable 2025-02-10).
- Work with procurement to confirm retention & purge SLAs; Lena to share internal questionnaire by 2025-02-06.
- Start hosted POC integration by 2025-02-07 once dev keys provisioned.

Conversation notes / quotes:
- Marcus (CTO): "We need predictable latency for support assistants — can't have noisy neighbors in production."
- Lena (Head of Security): "SOC2 and SSO are table stakes; beyond that it's logging and the ability to delete user data on request."

Risks / unknowns:
- POC success may not translate to Dedicated if VPC networking introduces latency or added overhead.
- Procurement timeline stretches if SOC2 evidence delayed.

Other links & artifacts:
- Hosted demo recording: drive:/RD-Demos/Silverbloom/hosted-demo-2025-01-22.mp4
- Pricing sketch: drive:/RD-Deals/Silverbloom/pricing-sketch-2025-01-22.xlsx

CRM-style short fragments:
- "Lena wants SOC2 + log export"
- "Marcus asked about token-level cost breakdowns"
- "Priya to run canary for embeddings latency"

title: Astraeus Realtime Labs
preview:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput during market open windows (13:30-14:30 ET) - want reserved capacity to avoid noisy-neighbor impact.

SLA negotiation highlights: they insist on financial credits for downtime (tiered), 99.95% as baseline target proposed by them, pushing for 99.99% for core inference path. Must have <1 minute failover to fallback model variant. Require 72-hour maintenance notice, 2x per month maintenance window only outside 13:00-15:00 ET.

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. Legal wants right to audit infra configurations quarterly.

Procurement notes (shorthand): "legal: credit schedule, termination on 2 consecutive SLA breaches, cap on credits = monthly fee"; procurement wants sample contract redlines from Redwood.

Quote from CTO on 2024-11-21 call: "We need predictability — if it slips during opens we lose millions. Credits are good, but we need guaranteed reserve and fast remediation."

Attachments: pricing deck v3, rough perf-run CSV, SOC2 gap analysis spreadsheet.

title: Lighthouse MedTransit
preview: Stage: evaluation. Short: large enterprise healthcare payments operator; processes claims + card refunds (PCI + PHI). Primary ask: agent-assist for contact center + accurate ticket summarization, plus secure tool-calls to payment vault (no PAN in prompts). Reliability under spikes is critical (monthly billing cycles + claim surges). Security: Okta SSO + SCIM, require audited KMS with HSM, audit logging retained 7yrs, strict egress controls from VPC, SOC2/ISO artifacts before procurement. POC details: 4-week pilot; sanitized production chat logs (scrubbed PANs) to validate summarization + action flow. Success metrics: reduce avg handle time (AHT) by 30%, >95% summary accuracy on top-2 intents, 0 incidents in security review. Quotes: "Can't have raw PANs leave our VPC" (Head of Ops), "Need cryptographic proof of key separation and HSM-backed KMS" (Security). Integrations: Okta, internal payment vault API (tool-calls via signed short-lived tokens), SIEM ingest for audit logs, regional data residency (US + EU shards). Open items: confirm dedicated VPC peering path, finalize SOW, obtain SOC2 Type II report and PCI SAQ details. Support tickets: raised RED-1245 about tool-calling latency; SEC-2311 for encryption/key rotation questions. Next steps: security sign-off, supply test dataset & SOW, pilot kickoff estimated 2025-01-10.

title: Larkspur Trust Technologies
preview:
Qualified enterprise account. Primary contact: CTO Mira Patel (security-first). Wants private VPC deployment with customer-controlled keys (HSM-backed preferred). Key points from 11/07 demo:
- CTO: "No platform-managed key material for sensitive models" — insists on BYOK and HSM wrapping.
- Security: provided preliminary key-policy checklist; asked for KMS call flow diagram and key rotation SLA.
- Infra: wants vault integration options (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP KMS) + offline HSM for on-prem DR.
- Performance sensitivity: low-latency chat; target p95 < 120ms for 512-token replies in US east region.
- Cost: enterprise budget but wants Redwood Optimize suggestions for quantization and batching to reduce GPU hours.
- Compliance: data residency US-only, require audit logs forwarded to SIEM, 7y retention for audit trails.
- Quote from Mira on call: "We cannot accept vendor KMS unless wrapped by an HSM and attested."
POC plan sketched: initial bench with a non-sensitive model -> BYOK key test -> full encrypted inference demo -> SOC/pen testing.
Typical CRM shorthand in notes: security questionnaire submitted 11/01, sent KMS integration spec 11/04, legal asked for BYOK contract language 11/10.
Risks noted: legal redlines on key custody, network egress constraints with existing firewall rules, potential need for on-prem HSM appliance.
Suggested Redwood playbook items: provide KMS integration guide for each cloud (diagram + example role policies), sample HSM attestation report, template BYOK contract language, and an initial key-rotation runbook.

title: Pinecrest Analytics
preview: Summary: strong product fit for personalization + reranking; technical validation done, team liked runtime stability. Primary barrier is unit economics. Finance says per-conversation token cost + projected GPU run-rate yields negative ROI compared to keeping in-house Distil-ensemble (current infra).

Finance says per-conversation token cost + projected GPU run-rate yields negative ROI compared to keeping in-house Distil-ensemble (current infra).

title: Meadowlark Interfaces
preview: SMB inbound from marketing widget; lightweight engineering org (frontend-heavy). Primary ask: 'We want a drop-in example to get knowledge base Q&A running on Vercel + Next.js using LangChain or LlamaIndex.' They prefer hosted_api for now (no Private). Key constraints: run from Vercel Edge / Serverless, low mem budget per function, latency target ~200-300ms for first token, embeddings throughput ~20-50 qps cost-conscious. Requests: JS/TS quickstart repo, Vercel AI adapter recommendation, sample prompt engineering for product-recs, migration notes for when they scale to Dedicated. Security: need SOC2 attestation and SSO plan (Okta). Quote: 'If you give us a one-click copyable example that works with Next.js + Vercel we can roll it into our codebase this week.' Timeline: POC by end of month. Next: send repo, confirm time for walkthrough. Contacts: CTO Maya Lin, Frontend lead Aaron Cho (shared in call).

title: LexForte Compliance Hub
preview:
Account summary:
- LexForte is a mid-size legaltech provider focused on enterprise e-discovery and compliance automation. Looking to replace homegrown retrieval stack with RAG + reranking for higher precision on privileged/PII detection.

Requirements:
- Index scale: target initial index 25M docs, long-term 150M+ artifacts (PDFs, ESI, transcripts).
- Query volume: peak ~800 qps across tenants; average ~150 qps.
- Latency targets: embedding nearest-neighbor recall <200ms (interactive), end-to-end rerank <350-450ms for single-query interactive flows.
- Cost sensitivity: target <$0.03/query average across reranking path; willing to trade 10-15% latency for ~20% cost reduction via quantization/batching.
- Models: prefer open weights (Llama-family / Mistral variants) for private deployment; require validated quantized variants and documented quality regressions.
- Routing/fallback: per-customer routing (EU customers to EU region), policy-based fallback to smaller hosted model during capacity events.
- Security: VPC/private control plane, customer-managed KMS, per-request audit logs with exportable trails, immutable retention controls (legal hold), SSO (SAML), ability to produce regulator-ready audit exports.

Recent activity timeline:
- 2024-08-22: Initial inbound from CTO; AE assigned (Jordan).
- 2024-09-05: Discovery call (fireflies id FF-2024-09-05-LF): deep-dive on scale, noted 25M seed index.
- 2024-10-12: Tech workshop with SE (Priya) and infra eng; reviewed architecture draft; asked for quantization recommendation.
- 2024-10-28: Sent security questionnaire and sample audit log schema (drive link); legal asked for retention granularity options.
- 2024-11-01: Pricing deck sent; procurement requested POC statement of work.
- 2024-11-07: Security call with CISO; requested SAML metadata and cloud region residency guarantees.

POC plan (proposed):
- 2-week pilot: index 2-5M canonical docs (mix PDF, .msg, transcripts).
- Evaluate: recall@k, rerank precision, average latency, cost per 1k queries.
- Success criteria: recall improvement +15% vs existing, rerank precision +10% at <400ms median; cost <= $0.03/query.

Open questions & next steps:
- Confirm legal hold retention enforcement model (immutable vs soft-delete).
- Agree on encryption at rest with customer KMS across Dedicated/Private.
- Finalize SOC2/ISO artifacts and remote audit windows.
- Next: architecture review (invite infra + CISO) — target week of 2024-11-13.

Quote/paraphrase:
- CISO: "Audit trails are non-negotiable; we need immutable, query-level logs tied to SSO identity."
- CTO: "If we can hit <400ms and prove cost parity, we can consolidate search stack."

Links:
- drive/shared/lexforte/lexforte-redwood-pricing-v1.pdf
- drive/shared/lexforte/security-faq-lexforte-answers.docx
- Jira: RINF-3421 (POC infra)
- Gmail thread: thread:1753a2cdf_lexforte-procurement

title: Borealis SecureAI
preview:
- Initial interest: build IR assistant and internal doc search across incident logs.
- 2024-06-10 intro call: strong exec buy-in (CTO + Head of Product). AO: Jordan Hayes.
- 2024-07-21 demo: showed private deployment flow + latency/cost estimates. Customer asked for on-prem options and KMS integration.
- Security team responded 2024-08: requires full architecture whitepaper, penetration testing plan, and table-top audit before any POC.
- Key quote from Head of Security: "We cannot start a proof-of-concept until we validate offline execution and HSM key control."
- SE (Priya) supplied architecture doc and quantization notes 2024-08-05; security requested longer retention controls and proof of SOC2 evidence.
- Internal champion (Principal ML Engineer, Diego Alvarez) left the company 2024-09; project lost momentum.
- 2024-09-12 finance flagged hiring/budget freeze; procurement deprioritized infra spends until Q2.
- Competitor note: customer ran a pilot with a hyperscaler-managed endpoint (lower friction) during evaluation; leadership preferred turnkey SLA.
- Current status: POC not started — couldn't align on success criteria, security gating and resourcing.
- Suggested re-engage cadence: reconnect in 2025-Q2 after budget cycle; offer a pre-approved security pack and a time-limited evaluation SLA.
- Owner comment: "Good technical fit but organizational timing is the blocker. Will archive and attempt warm re-open later."
